links.targetTitle: SwarmChannel implements IChannel so the reactor SyncManager is transport-agnostic
Connect's Swarm integration is split into three layers: bee-reactor-adapter, SwarmChannel, and Connect UI

global.title: SwarmChannel is pure transport; ordering, conflict resolution, and state computation stay in the reactor
global.content:
# SwarmChannel Is Transport Only

The SwarmChannel abstraction is deliberately thin. It does not interpret operation semantics or resolve conflicts.

## What SwarmChannel does

- Uploads `SyncEnvelope` payloads to `/bytes`
- Appends feed index pointers to the outbox feed
- Polls peer feeds for new entries
- Downloads referenced `/bytes` payloads into the inbox
- Routes undeliverable or malformed messages to a deadLetter queue

## What SwarmChannel does NOT do

- Determine final operation order
- Merge conflicting edits
- Deduplicate operations
- Compute document state

All of those responsibilities stay with the reactor's existing `SyncManager` and document model reducer — the same components that already handle sync in other transports (GraphQL, local storage, etc.).

## Why this matters

Because ordering/conflict/dedup logic lives in the reactor, adding Swarm as a transport does not require bespoke merge code. Any channel that can move `SyncEnvelope` data satisfies the contract.
global.description: SwarmChannel does not perform conflict resolution or ordering; it is pure transport moving SyncEnvelope data between feeds and reactor mailboxes (inbox/outbox/deadLetter).
